creator: chris@cretaforce.gr
text:
Many people likely set up a DNS server and MySQL on the same machine, so we need a more efficient solution. Some might not have attempted upgrades yet, possibly due to summer vacations, and thus may be unaware of this bug.

I attempted an upgrade using `portmaster` instead of the `pkg` system, but the build failed, leaving me on MySQL 8.0.35.

Here’s the output from `ldd` for MySQL 8.0.35:

libprotobuf-lite.so.3.19.4 => /usr/local/libexec/../lib/mysql/private/libprotobuf-lite.so.3.19.4 (0x1bb577222000)

For comparison, here’s the ldd output from comment #0, which I believe is from after upgrading MySQL to 8.0.39 using the pkg system:

libprotobuf-lite.so.24.4.0 => /usr/local/lib/libprotobuf-lite.so.24.4.0 (0x3fe81b9ab000)

The first `ldd` indicates that it's linked with the internal MySQL-specific version of libprotobuf located within the MySQL private library directory.

The second `ldd` output shows that it is linked with the protobuf library installed by protobuf port.

Based on comment #17, it seems that the error didn’t occur before because the version numbers of `libprotobuf-lite.so` differed between MySQL's bundled protobuf and the system’s (port) protobuf. Do you have any insights into why, in this case, it prefers the system’s (port) protobuf over the bundled one?
